g.title: Rigid disk added to a rotor node. Contributes mass and moments of inertia. Can be created by direct inertia or geometry.
Ip > Id → "thin" disk (gyroscopic effect stiffens) Ip < Id → "long" disk (gyroscopic effect softens) The Ip/Id ratio determines the behavior of critical speeds in the Campbell diagram.
DiskElement(n, m, Id, Ip) n: node where the disk is located m: disk mass [kg] Id: diametral moment of inertia [kg·m²] Ip: polar moment of inertia [kg·m²] Used when inertias are already known.

DiskElement.from_geometry(n, material, width, i_d, o_d) n: node where the disk is located material: Material object (for ρ) width: disk width/thickness [m] i_d: inner diameter [m] o_d: outer diameter [m] Automatically calculates m, Id and Ip.
m = ρ · π · width · (o_d² - i_d²) / 4 Mass from geometry and density
Ip = m · (o_d² + i_d²) / 8 Polar moment of inertia (resistance to rotor spin)
Id = m · (3·(o_d² + i_d²) + 4·width²) / 48 Diametral moment of inertia (resistance to tilting)
Mass matrix [4×4] Diagonal: [m, m, Id, Id] Translational mass and diametral inertia
Stiffness matrix [4×4] Null matrix (rigid disk)
Damping matrix [4×4] Null matrix (rigid disk)
Gyroscopic matrix [4×4] Gyroscopic effect proportional to Ip Couples rotations θx and θy
Rigid disk added to a rotor node. Contributes mass and moments of inertia. Can be created by direct inertia or geometry.
Ip > Id → "thin" disk (gyroscopic effect stiffens) Ip < Id → "long" disk (gyroscopic effect softens) The Ip/Id ratio determines the behavior of critical speeds in the Campbell diagram.
DiskElement(n, m, Id, Ip) n: node where the disk is located m: disk mass [kg] Id: diametral moment of inertia [kg·m²] Ip: polar moment of inertia [kg·m²] Used when inertias are already known.

DiskElement.from_geometry(n, material, width, i_d, o_d) n: node where the disk is located material: Material object (for ρ) width: disk width/thickness [m] i_d: inner diameter [m] o_d: outer diameter [m] Automatically calculates m, Id and Ip.
m = ρ · π · width · (o_d² - i_d²) / 4 Mass from geometry and density
Ip = m · (o_d² + i_d²) / 8 Polar moment of inertia (resistance to rotor spin)
Id = m · (3·(o_d² + i_d²) + 4·width²) / 48 Diametral moment of inertia (resistance to tilting)
Mass matrix [4×4] Diagonal: [m, m, Id, Id] Translational mass and diametral inertia
Stiffness matrix [4×4] Null matrix (rigid disk)
Damping matrix [4×4] Null matrix (rigid disk)
